findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 28. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: Ensure the main landmark is at top level
findings.evidence:
Fix any of the following:
  The main landmark is contained in another landmark. [<main>]

findings.description: Ensure the document has at most one main landmark
findings.evidence:
Fix any of the following:
  Document has more than one main landmark [<main>]

Fix any of the following:
  The landmark must have a unique aria-label, aria-labelledby, or title to make landmarks distinguishable [<main>]

findings.description: Repeated page header/footer content in HTML
findings.evidence: The following text blocks repeat 3+ times, likely page headers/footers that should not appear in the body: 'where,' (3x).

findings.description: Section headings in the PDF have underlined text (e.g., '2.1 Outfall 002 Sample Types', '2.2 River Intake Samples', etc.) which is removed in the HTML. However, underline removal is acceptable per the rules. The headings still have bold formatting preserved.
findings.evidence: This is not actually a finding - underline removal is acceptable. Withdrawing.

findings.description: Section headings for major numbered sections use inconsistent heading levels. Sections 1 and 2 use <h1> tags, but their subsections (2.1, 2.2, etc.) use <h2>. The LIST OF TABLES and LIST OF FIGURES headings under the TOC use <h3>. Meanwhile, the LIST OF ABBREVIATIONS uses <h2>. The TABLES and FIGURES divider pages use <h1>. This creates a somewhat inconsistent hierarchy, though the main issue is that Section 2 top-level heading uses <h1> while its subsections correctly use <h2>, which is reasonable. However, Section 3 top-level heading uses <h1> but its subsections also use <h2>, which is consistent. The real issue is that the 'LIST OF TABLES' and 'LIST OF FIGURES' are demoted to <h3> while in the PDF they appear as visually similar-level headings to 'LIST OF ABBREVIATIONS' which is <h2>.
findings.evidence: PDF: LIST OF TABLES, LIST OF FIGURES, and LIST OF ABBREVIATIONS are all similar-level section headings. HTML: LIST OF TABLES and LIST OF FIGURES are <h3>, while LIST OF ABBREVIATIONS is <h2>.

findings.description: The Geosyntec consultants logo image appears multiple times in the HTML body mid-content (before TABLES section and before FIGURES section), replicating the PDF page headers/logos. While consolidation would be expected, these appear mid-content and are acceptable as section dividers. However, the cover page content with the professional seal and Geosyntec logo information is essentially duplicated - appearing both in the cover page div and being part of the continuous document flow.
findings.evidence: Multiple
